SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.util.fire

Class BlockedInLiquidExpansionAnalysis

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.util.fire.BlockedInLiquidExpansionAnalysis
public final class BlockedInLiquidExpansionAnalysis extends Object
Rigorous, equation-of-state based screening tool for the pressure rise generated when a liquid-full piping segment or vessel is blocked in (isolated with no vapor space and no relief path) and subsequently warmed, per API 521 7th Ed. Section 4.4.12 ("Liquid Thermal Expansion").

Blocked-in liquid thermal expansion is one of the most severe overpressure scenarios in process plants: because liquids are nearly incompressible, even a modest temperature rise can produce extreme pressure increases unless a thermal relief device is installed. The simplified screening relation

dP = (beta / kappa) * dT

(where beta is the isobaric thermal expansion coefficient and kappa is the isothermal compressibility) is only strictly valid for an infinitesimal temperature step at fixed reference properties. This class supplements that simplified relation with a rigorous isochoric (constant fluid density, i.e. constant volume for a fixed mass and fixed pipe/vessel volume) pressure march that re-flashes the fluid at each temperature step and searches for the pressure that reproduces the reference density recorded at the initial blocked-in state.

This class is a screening tool. It does not replace a thermal relief valve sizing study (see ReliefValveSizing) and does not account for vapor space, trace heating, insulation credit, or non-uniform heating along a pipe run.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private static final double
    DENSITY_RELATIVE_TOLERANCE
    Relative density tolerance used to accept a bisection solution.
    private static final int
    MAX_BISECTION_ITERATIONS
    Maximum number of bisection iterations per temperature point.
    private static final int
    MAX_BRACKET_EXPANSIONS
    Maximum number of pressure-bracket expansion steps per temperature point.
    private static final double
    MAX_SEARCH_PRESSURE_PA
    Upper bound of the pressure search range, in Pa (10 000 bara).
    private static final double
    MIN_SEARCH_PRESSURE_PA
    Lower bound of the pressure search range, in Pa (0.01 bara).
    private static final double
    PA_PER_BARA
    Conversion factor from bara to Pa.

  • Constructor Details

    • BlockedInLiquidExpansionAnalysis

      private BlockedInLiquidExpansionAnalysis()
      Private constructor. This is a static utility class and must not be instantiated.

  • Method Details

    • computeIsochoricPressureProfile

      public static double[] computeIsochoricPressureProfile(SystemInterface fluid, double[] temperaturesK)
      Computes the isochoric (constant-density) pressure profile of a blocked-in liquid as it is warmed through a sequence of temperatures.

      The reference density is taken from the supplied fluid at its current temperature and pressure (the initial blocked-in state). For every requested temperature the method performs a bracket-and-bisection search on pressure, re-flashing a clone of the fluid at each trial pressure, until the resulting mixture density matches the reference density to within a relative tolerance of 1.0E-6.

      Parameters:
      fluid - the fluid at the initial blocked-in state; its current temperature and pressure are used to compute the reference (constant) density. The fluid itself is not modified; all flashes are performed on internal clones.
      temperaturesK - the sequence of temperatures, in Kelvin, at which to evaluate the isochoric pressure (does not need to be sorted, but is typically monotonically increasing for a heating scenario)
      Returns:
      an array, the same length as temperaturesK, of isochoric pressures in Pa
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null or temperaturesK is null or empty
      IllegalStateException - if a pressure bracket containing the reference density cannot be found within the configured search bounds (for example because the fluid leaves the single liquid-phase region over the requested temperature range)

    • estimateThermalExpansionCoefficient

      public static double estimateThermalExpansionCoefficient(SystemInterface fluid, double dT)
      Estimates the isobaric thermal expansion coefficient, beta = -(1/rho)*(d rho / dT)_P, of the supplied fluid by central finite differences at constant pressure.
      Parameters:
      fluid - the fluid at the reference temperature and pressure; not modified
      dT - the temperature step used for the central difference, in Kelvin, must be positive
      Returns:
      the estimated thermal expansion coefficient, in 1/K
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null or dT is not positive

    • estimateIsothermalCompressibility

      public static double estimateIsothermalCompressibility(SystemInterface fluid, double dP)
      Estimates the isothermal compressibility, kappa = (1/rho)*(d rho / dP)_T, of the supplied fluid by central finite differences at constant temperature.
      Parameters:
      fluid - the fluid at the reference temperature and pressure; not modified
      dP - the pressure step used for the central difference, in Pa, must be positive
      Returns:
      the estimated isothermal compressibility, in 1/Pa
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null or dP is not positive

    • simplifiedPressureRise

      public static double simplifiedPressureRise(double beta, double kappa, double deltaT)
      Computes the simplified, constant-property thermal expansion pressure rise, dP = (beta / kappa) * dT, per API 521 Section 4.4.12.

      This relation is only accurate for small temperature steps around the reference state at which beta and kappa were evaluated; for larger temperature changes prefer computeIsochoricPressureProfile(SystemInterface, double[]).

      Parameters:
      beta - the isobaric thermal expansion coefficient, in 1/K
      kappa - the isothermal compressibility, in 1/Pa, must be positive
      deltaT - the temperature rise, in K
      Returns:
      the estimated pressure rise, in Pa
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if kappa is not positive

    • validateFluid

      private static void validateFluid(SystemInterface fluid)
      Validates that a fluid argument is usable by this class.
      Parameters:
      fluid - the fluid to validate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null

    • densityAt

      private static double densityAt(SystemInterface template, double temperatureK, double pressurePa)
      Flashes a clone of the supplied fluid template at the given temperature and pressure and returns the resulting mixture density.
      Parameters:
      template - the fluid whose composition and model are cloned for the flash
      temperatureK - the flash temperature, in Kelvin
      pressurePa - the flash pressure, in Pa
      Returns:
      the mixture density at the flashed state, in kg/m3

    • solvePressureForDensity

      private static double solvePressureForDensity(SystemInterface template, double temperatureK, double targetDensityKgPerM3, double startPressurePa)
      Searches for the pressure at the given temperature that reproduces the target density, using bracket expansion followed by bisection.
      Parameters:
      template - the fluid whose composition and model are cloned for each trial flash
      temperatureK - the temperature at which to search, in Kelvin
      targetDensityKgPerM3 - the target (reference) density, in kg/m3
      startPressurePa - an initial guess for the pressure search, in Pa (typically the previously solved pressure at an adjacent temperature)
      Returns:
      the pressure, in Pa, at which the flashed density matches the target density to within 1.0E-6 relative
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if a bracket containing the target density cannot be found within the configured search bounds
